<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.

payload.message:
# Context from my IDE setup:

## Active file: src/services/HotelService.ts

## Active selection of the file:
syncBookingCommissionForTargetAgency
## Open tabs:
- HotelService.ts: src/services/HotelService.ts
- HotelCommissionService.ts: src/services/HotelCommissionService.ts
- HotelBookingService.ts: src/services/HotelBookingService.ts
- HotelController.ts: src/rests/controllers/HotelController.ts
- HotelBookingInput.ts: src/services/types/HotelBookingInput.ts

## My request for Codex:
tôi muốn update để lưu thông tin comm được chia tỷ lệ bên ats-api này, còn call sang bên source thì vẫn giữ nguyên

Nên làm theo mô hình 2 lớp dữ liệu:

- dữ liệu gửi sang source/orch: giữ nguyên commission gốc
- dữ liệu lưu ở `ats-api`: lưu commission đã chia theo tỷ lệ F2

Cụ thể flow nên là:

1. Trước khi call source
- lấy ra `sourceCommission` của từng room
- chuẩn hóa vào một field riêng, ví dụ:
  - `room.commissionFromSource = commission gốc`
  - `room.commissionTotal = commission gốc`
- call sang orch bằng `commissionTotal = commission gốc`
- tức là payload outbound không bị giảm về 41%

2. Sau khi có context agency đích ở `ats-api`
- tính `distributedCommission = sourceCommission * 41%`
- lưu phần này vào DB của mình:
  - `totalCommissionAmount` = tổng commission F2
  - `bookingRooms[].commissionTotal` = commission F2
  - `commissionFromSource` ở booking = tổng commission gốc từ source
- như vậy DB của mình phản ánh “phần được chia cho F2”, còn source vẫn giữ số gốc

3. Điểm quan trọng
- không nên mutate `data.rooms[].commissionTotal` về F2 trước khi gọi orch
- chỉ nên tạo một map/tạm biến trong BE:
  - `sourceRoomCommissions`
  - `distributedRoomCommissions`
- outbound dùng source
- persist dùng distributed

4. Cách tổ chức code
- `syncBookingCommissionForTargetAgency()` không nên sửa payload để gửi source
- nó nên chỉ trả về metadata kiểu:
  - `roomCommissionFromSourceByRoomId`
  - `distributedCommissionByRoomId`
  - `distributedTotalCommission`
- rồi trong `createBooking()`:
  - call `integrationService.createBooking(data)` với payload gốc
  - sau khi nhận booking response, lúc build `bookingRooms` để lưu DB thì thay `commissionTotal` bằng giá trị distributed
  - `commissionFromSource` vẫn lấy từ source response hoặc map gốc

5. Kết quả business
- source/orchestrator luôn nhận full commission gốc
- `ats-api` lưu phần commission đã chia theo F2
- về sau payment/report ở `ats-api` sẽ chạy theo phần F2 mà anh muốn hiển thị/quản trị

Nếu anh muốn, bước tiếp theo tôi sẽ sửa code theo đúng hướng này:
- bỏ việc ghi đè `room.commissionTotal` trước khi gọi orch
- đổi `syncBookingCommissionForTargetAgency()` thành hàm chỉ tính toán và trả metadata
- dùng metadata đó để lưu DB commission theo tỷ lệ trong `ats-api` thôi.

1. Trước khi call source
- lấy ra `sourceCommission` của từng room
- chuẩn hóa vào một field riêng, ví dụ:
  - `room.commissionFromSource = commission gốc`
  - `room.commissionTotal = commission gốc`
- call sang orch bằng `commissionTotal = commission gốc`
- tức là payload outbound không bị giảm về 41%

2. Sau khi có context agency đích ở `ats-api`
- tính `distributedCommission = sourceCommission * 41%`
- lưu phần này vào DB của mình:
  - `totalCommissionAmount` = tổng commission F2
  - `bookingRooms[].commissionTotal` = commission F2
  - `commissionFromSource` ở booking = tổng commission gốc từ source
- như vậy DB của mình phản ánh “phần được chia cho F2”, còn source vẫn giữ số gốc

3. Điểm quan trọng
- không nên mutate `data.rooms[].commissionTotal` về F2 trước khi gọi orch
- chỉ nên tạo một map/tạm biến trong BE:
  - `sourceRoomCommissions`
  - `distributedRoomCommissions`
- outbound dùng source
- persist dùng distributed

4. Cách tổ chức code
- `syncBookingCommissionForTargetAgency()` không nên sửa payload để gửi source
- nó nên chỉ trả về metadata kiểu:
  - `roomCommissionFromSourceByRoomId`
  - `distributedCommissionByRoomId`
  - `distributedTotalCommission`
- rồi trong `createBooking()`:
  - call `integrationService.createBooking(data)` với payload gốc
  - sau khi nhận booking response, lúc build `bookingRooms` để lưu DB thì thay `commissionTotal` bằng giá trị distributed
  - `commissionFromSource` vẫn lấy từ source response hoặc map gốc

5. Kết quả business
- source/orchestrator luôn nhận full commission gốc
- `ats-api` lưu phần commission đã chia theo F2
- về sau payment/report ở `ats-api` sẽ chạy theo phần F2 mà anh muốn hiển thị/quản trị

Nếu anh muốn, bước tiếp theo tôi sẽ sửa code theo đúng hướng này:
- bỏ việc ghi đè `room.commissionTotal` trước khi gọi orch
- đổi `syncBookingCommissionForTargetAgency()` thành hàm chỉ tính toán và trả metadata
- dùng metadata đó để lưu DB commission theo tỷ lệ trong `ats-api` thôi.

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

Tôi đã sửa [`HotelService.ts`](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/1-ats/ats-api/src/services/HotelService.ts) theo hướng:

- `syncBookingCommissionForTargetAgency()` giờ tính lại toàn bộ commission cho case F1 book hộ F2 ở cả:
  - `room.commissionFromSource`
  - `room.commissionTotal`
  - `stayDate.commissionFromSource`
  - `stayDate.commissionRate`
  - `room.commissions[].commissionRate`
  - `stayDate.commissions[].commissionRate`
  - tổng `totalCommissionAmount` ở cấp booking metadata
- helper này trả về:
  - tổng commission F2 đã normalize
  - map commission theo `roomId`
- `createBooking()` dùng đúng tổng/map đó để lưu dữ liệu commission phía `ats-api`

Đồng thời tôi giữ đúng rule anh muốn:
- payload call sang source/orch vẫn dùng commission gốc
- dữ liệu lưu trong `ats-api` dùng commission đã chia theo tỷ lệ F2

Tôi đã thêm `buildSourceBookingPayload()` để trước khi gọi orch:
- restore lại `commissionTotal`/`commissionRate` về source value
- loại bỏ các field tạm `__source...`

`yarn check:type` đã pass.

Behavior sau khi sửa:
- F1 book cho F2 với cấu hình `41%`
- source vẫn nhận full commission gốc
- DB bên `ats-api` sẽ lưu toàn bộ commission-related fields theo 41% của F2

Nếu anh muốn, bước tiếp theo tôi có thể sửa luôn phần update ở `getBooking()` để khi sync lại từ orch, các field nested commission ở `bookingRooms.stayDates` cũng giữ theo bản F2 trong DB thay vì chỉ giữ `room.commissionTotal`.

- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

đây là booking sau khi tôi book, thì gặp vấn đề như sau

bookingRooms[0].commissionTotal = 50k
bookingRooms[0].stayDates[0].commissionRate = 50k

thưc chất nó chỉ bằng totalCommissionAmount = 20500 thôi

bạn kiểm tra xem đoạn book tôi lưu sai hay do đoạn retrieve về bị sai nhé

Tôi sẽ kiểm tra cả 2 điểm: dữ liệu được lưu lúc `create` và dữ liệu bị ghi đè lúc `getBooking` sync từ orch. Với symptom anh đưa ra, khả năng cao lỗi nằm ở đoạn retrieve đang overwrite nested commission từ `raw` hơn là lúc insert ban đầu.

content.text: Tôi sẽ kiểm tra cả 2 điểm: dữ liệu được lưu lúc `create` và dữ liệu bị ghi đè lúc `getBooking` sync từ orch. Với symptom anh đưa ra, khả năng cao lỗi nằm ở đoạn retrieve đang overwrite nested commission từ `raw` hơn là lúc insert ban đầu.

payload.message:
Lỗi nằm ở đoạn `retrieve`, không phải phần `book` là chính.

Dấu hiệu:
- `totalCommissionAmount = 20500`
- `totalPriceToBePaid = 669500`

2 field này chứng tỏ lúc lưu booking bên `ats-api` anh đã có dữ liệu F2 đúng rồi. Nếu đoạn create lưu sai thì 2 field tổng này cũng đã sai theo.

Chỗ làm sai là [`HotelService.ts`](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/1-ats/ats-api/src/services/HotelService.ts#L239) trong `getBooking()`:

```ts
bookingRooms: bookingRes.bookingRooms.map(room => {
  const existingRoom = item.bookingRooms?.find(r => r.id === room.id);
  const commissionTotal =
    !isF1 && existingRoom?.commissionTotal != null ? existingRoom.commissionTotal : room.commissionTotal;
  return {
    ...room,
    commissionTotal,
    ...
  };
}),
```

Vấn đề cụ thể:
- Nếu F1 gọi `getBooking`, `isF1 = true` nên code lấy luôn `room.commissionTotal` từ `bookingRes` của orch, tức là `50000`
- Ngay cả khi F2 gọi, code hiện tại cũng chỉ giữ lại `room.commissionTotal`, còn:
  - `stayDates[].commissionRate`
  - `stayDates[].commissionFromSource`
  - `commissions[]`
  vẫn bị overwrite từ orch về giá trị source `50000`

Nên với sample anh gửi:
- `bookingRooms[0].commissionTotal = 50000`
- `bookingRooms[0].stayDates[0].commissionRate = 50000`

là do `getBooking()` sync lại từ source sai, không phải do dữ liệu normalize lúc create hoàn toàn không chạy.

Tóm lại:
- `create`: nhìn theo tổng số tiền thì đang lưu đúng phần F2
- `retrieve`: đang trả sai vì overwrite nested commission từ `bookingRes`

Cách sửa đúng:
- trong `getBooking()` phải merge `bookingRes.bookingRooms` với `item.bookingRooms` từ DB
- ưu tiên giữ commission-related fields từ DB cho booking book hộ:
  - `commissionTotal`
  - `commissionFromSource`
  - `stayDates[].commissionRate`
  - `stayDates[].commissionFromSource`
  - `commissions[]`
  - `stayDates[].commissions[]`
- chỉ lấy từ orch các field trạng thái runtime như:
  - `status`
  - `paymentDeadline`
  - `paymentHistories`
  - `cancellation`

Nếu anh muốn, tôi sửa tiếp ngay đoạn `getBooking()` theo hướng merge đúng toàn bộ nested commission fields.

là do `getBooking()` sync lại từ source sai, không phải do dữ liệu normalize lúc create hoàn toàn không chạy.

Tóm lại:
- `create`: nhìn theo tổng số tiền thì đang lưu đúng phần F2
- `retrieve`: đang trả sai vì overwrite nested commission từ `bookingRes`

Cách sửa đúng:
- trong `getBooking()` phải merge `bookingRes.bookingRooms` với `item.bookingRooms` từ DB
- ưu tiên giữ commission-related fields từ DB cho booking book hộ:
  - `commissionTotal`
  - `commissionFromSource`
  - `stayDates[].commissionRate`
  - `stayDates[].commissionFromSource`
  - `commissions[]`
  - `stayDates[].commissions[]`
- chỉ lấy từ orch các field trạng thái runtime như:
  - `status`
  - `paymentDeadline`
  - `paymentHistories`
  - `cancellation`

Nếu anh muốn, tôi sửa tiếp ngay đoạn `getBooking()` theo hướng merge đúng toàn bộ nested commission fields.

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

Tôi đã sửa ở [`HotelService.ts`](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/1-ats/ats-api/src/services/HotelService.ts) để `getBooking()` không retrieve commission từ orch nữa.

Hiện tại khi sync booking:
- giữ commission từ DB cho `bookingRooms[].commissionTotal`
- giữ commission từ DB cho `bookingRooms[].commissionFromSource`
- giữ commission từ DB cho `bookingRooms[].stayDates[].commissionRate`
- giữ commission từ DB cho `bookingRooms[].stayDates[].commissionFromSource`
- giữ `commissions[]` từ DB

Còn các field runtime khác như `status`, `paymentDeadline`, `paymentHistories`, `cancellation` vẫn lấy từ orch như trước.

Tôi cũng đã chạy `yarn check:type` và pass.

Với case anh đưa, sau sửa này:
- `bookingRooms[0].commissionTotal` sẽ giữ `20500`
- `bookingRooms[0].stayDates[0].commissionRate` cũng sẽ giữ theo dữ liệu DB đã normalize, không bị orch đè về `50000` nữa

Nếu anh muốn, tôi có thể sửa tiếp `getBookings()` hoặc các chỗ sync khác để thống nhất luôn việc “không pull commission từ orch” trên toàn flow hotel.
